entries.label: Immutable binding
entries.code: let x = 5;
entries.note: Cannot be reassigned after initialization

entries.label: Mutable binding
entries.code:
let mut x = 5;
x = 10;
entries.note: Use mut keyword to allow reassignment

entries.label: Type annotation
entries.code: let x: i32 = 5;
entries.note: Explicit type after colon

entries.label: Shadowing
entries.note: Re-declare with let; can change type

entries.label: Constant
entries.code: const MAX_POINTS: u32 = 100_000;
entries.note: Must have type annotation; evaluated at compile time

entries.label: Static variable
entries.code: static GREETING: &str = "Hello";
entries.note: Fixed memory address; lives for entire program

entries.label: Destructuring let
entries.code: let (x, y, z) = (1, 2.0, "three");
entries.note: Unpack tuples into separate bindings

entries.label: Unused variable
entries.code: let _unused = 42;
entries.note: Prefix with _ to suppress unused warnings

entries.label: Integers
entries.note: Signed: i8, i16, i32, i64, i128, isize. Unsigned: u8, u16, u32, u64, u128, usize

entries.label: Floats
entries.note: f64 is the default; f32 for less precision

entries.label: Boolean
entries.code:
let t: bool = true;
let f = false;
entries.note: true or false; 1 byte in size

entries.label: Character
entries.code:
let c: char = 'z';
let heart = '\u{2764}';
entries.note: 4 bytes; represents a Unicode scalar value

entries.label: Unit type
entries.code: let unit: () = ();
entries.note: Empty tuple; returned by functions with no return value

entries.label: Tuple
entries.code:
let tup: (i32, f64, bool) = (500, 6.4, true);
let five_hundred = tup.0;
entries.note: Fixed-length collection of different types; access by index

entries.label: Array
entries.note: Fixed-length, same type; [value; count] for repeated values

entries.label: Never type
entries.note: ! indicates a function never returns

entries.label: Basic function
entries.note: Parameters must have type annotations

entries.label: Return value
entries.note: Last expression without semicolon is the return value

entries.label: Early return
entries.note: Use return keyword for early exit

entries.label: Generic function
entries.note: Type parameter T with trait bound

entries.label: Multiple return values
entries.note: Return a tuple and destructure at call site

entries.label: Function pointer
entries.note: fn(...) -> ... type for function pointers

entries.label: if / else
entries.note: Condition must be bool; no parentheses required

entries.label: if as expression
entries.code: let val = if condition { "yes" } else { "no" };
entries.note: Both branches must return the same type

entries.label: loop
entries.note: Infinite loop; break can return a value

entries.label: while loop
entries.note: Loop while condition is true

entries.label: for loop
entries.note: Iterate over any type implementing IntoIterator

entries.label: Loop labels
entries.note: Label loops to break/continue outer loops

entries.label: while let
entries.note: Loop while pattern matches; great for Option/Result

entries.label: Define struct
entries.note: Named fields with types

entries.label: Create instance
entries.code: let p = Point { x: 1.0, y: 2.0 };
entries.note: All fields must be initialized

entries.label: Field init shorthand
entries.note: When variable name matches field name

entries.label: Struct update syntax
entries.code: let p2 = Point { x: 5.0, ..p };
entries.note: Copy remaining fields from another instance

entries.label: Tuple struct
entries.code:
struct Color(u8, u8, u8);
let red = Color(255, 0, 0);
entries.note: Named tuple; fields accessed by index (red.0)

entries.label: Unit struct
entries.code:
struct Marker;
let m = Marker;
entries.note: No fields; useful for trait implementations

entries.label: Impl block
entries.note: Methods and associated functions defined in impl blocks

entries.label: Simple enum
entries.note: Variants are namespaced under the enum name

entries.label: Enum with data
entries.note: Variants can hold different data types

entries.label: Using enum
entries.code:
let msg = Message::Write(String::from("hello"));
let dir = Direction::North;
entries.note: Access variants with :: syntax

entries.label: Impl on enum
entries.note: Enums can have methods just like structs

entries.label: Option enum
entries.code:
let some_val: Option<i32> = Some(42);
let no_val: Option<i32> = None;
entries.note: Rust's replacement for null; in the standard library

entries.label: Result enum
entries.code:
let ok: Result<i32, String> = Ok(42);
let err: Result<i32, String> = Err("fail".into());
entries.note: For operations that may fail; in the standard library

entries.label: Basic match
entries.note: Must be exhaustive; _ is a catch-all wildcard

entries.label: Match with binding
entries.note: Destructure enum variants in match arms

entries.label: Match guard
entries.note: Extra condition with if after the pattern

entries.label: Multiple patterns
entries.note: Use | to match any of several patterns

entries.label: if let
entries.note: Sugar for match with one arm + wildcard

entries.label: let else
entries.note: Irrefutable pattern or diverge; available since Rust 1.65

entries.label: Define module
entries.note: Items are private by default; pub makes them public

entries.label: Use statement
entries.code:
use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::io::{self, Read, Write};
entries.note: Bring items into scope; group with braces

entries.label: Re-export
entries.code: pub use self::inner::MyType;
entries.note: Make item available from this module's namespace

entries.label: File as module
entries.note: Declare module; compiler looks for the file

entries.label: Crate root
entries.code:
use crate::utils::helper;
use super::parent_module;
entries.note: crate:: for absolute path; super:: for parent module

entries.label: Pub(crate)
entries.code:
pub(crate) fn internal_fn() {}
pub(super) fn parent_visible() {}
entries.note: Restrict visibility to crate or parent module

entries.label: Type alias
entries.code:
type Kilometers = i32;
type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, MyError>;
entries.note: Creates a synonym; not a distinct type

entries.label: Newtype pattern
entries.code:
struct Meters(f64);
struct Seconds(f64);
entries.note: Wrapper struct for type safety; prevents mixing units

entries.label: Generic type alias
entries.code:
type Pair<T> = (T, T);
let p: Pair<i32> = (1, 2);
entries.note: Type aliases can be generic

entries.label: Line comment
entries.code: // This is a comment
entries.note: Single line comment

entries.label: Block comment
entries.code:
/* This is a
   block comment */
entries.note: Multi-line comment

entries.label: Doc comment
entries.note: /// generates documentation; supports Markdown; doc tests run with cargo test

entries.label: Module doc comment
entries.code:
//! This module provides math utilities.
//! Use it for basic arithmetic.
entries.note: //! documents the enclosing item (module or crate)
